title: Network Security
overview: Module 3 takes you onto the wire. You'll learn network addressing, capture and analyze packets with Wireshark, and then connect to a real Azure-hosted patient portal to perform a live network analysis — the same kind of work an OCR auditor would expect to see documented.

units_breakdown.labDesc: CIDR, subnetting, interface identification. Define your attack surface before you scan it.
Wireshark deep dive on NEXUS training PCAPs. Display filters, TCP streams, endpoint statistics, IO graphs.

units_breakdown.labTitle: Lab 3.3: Network Analysis Engagement (Azure Live Capture)
units_breakdown.labDesc: Client engagement. Connect from Kali to the Gulf Coast patient portal in Azure, capture live traffic, and document tiered findings.

clientContext.label: Client Context: Gulf Coast Healthcare Partners
clientContext.body: Gulf Coast moved their patient portal to Azure (hosted by LoneStar DevOps) after the ransomware incident 18 months ago. With an OCR audit approaching, Jennifer Walsh (CISO) wants independent validation of the network traffic patterns to and from the portal. Thomas Kowalski (VP Infrastructure) made the call to move to cloud — he wants to see it was the right one.

stakeholders.name: Jennifer Walsh
stakeholders.title: CISO, Gulf Coast Healthcare
stakeholders.concern: The OCR auditors will ask about our cloud traffic. I need evidence that we know what’s flowing to Azure — not assumptions, evidence.

stakeholders.name: Thomas Kowalski
stakeholders.title: VP Infrastructure, Gulf Coast Healthcare
stakeholders.concern: After the ransomware, I moved everything I could to cloud. Tell me it was the right call — and tell me what I should be watching.

right: Live traffic capture against cloud targets

footer: Start with Lab 3.1 in the Modules section.
